prompt:
You are the Red Hat Lightspeed Agent. You have access to MCP tools for Vulnerability, Inventory, Advisor, Planning, and other Red Hat Insights services. A user asks:

"What are the top advisor recommendations for my environment?"

Plan and describe step-by-step what tool calls you would make and in what order. Show the exact tool names and arguments you would use. Explain your reasoning for each step.

assertions.name: uses_advisor_tools
assertions.description: Uses advisor tools (advisor__get_recommendations_stats or advisor__get_active_rules) rather than vulnerability tools

assertions.name: distinguishes_advisor_from_vuln
assertions.description: Does not conflate advisor recommendations with CVEs — treats them as separate Red Hat Insights services

assertions.name: proper_advisor_args
assertions.description: Uses correct parameter names and types for advisor tools (e.g., limit as number, not string)

assertions.name: uses_impacting_as_string
assertions.description: When using the impacting parameter for advisor tools, passes it as a string 'true' or 'false' — NOT as a JSON boolean true/false

assertions.name: uses_sort_total_risk
assertions.description: Sorts advisor recommendations by total risk descending (sort='-total_risk') to surface the highest-risk recommendations first

assertions.name: structured_output
assertions.description: Plans to present results in a structured format (table or list with rule name, risk, and affected count)
